General.question: When does the new academic year start?
General.answer: The new academic year begins on June 10th every year. Students are expected to report on the first day with all required stationery and books.

General.question: What are the school timings?
General.answer: School timings are from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM, Monday to Saturday. Students are advised to arrive by 7:50 AM to avoid being marked late.
variations: What time does school start and end?

General.question: Is school open tomorrow?
General.answer: School closure announcements are made through the official school app and SMS alerts. Please check the notice board or contact the school office at +91-XXXXXXXXXX for tomorrow's schedule.

General.question: When is the Parent-Teacher Meeting?
General.answer: PTM (Parent-Teacher Meeting) is held quarterly, typically after each term exam. Dates are communicated two weeks in advance via the school diary and the official app.

General.question: Is there school on Saturday?
General.answer: Yes, school is open on Saturdays from 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM for regular classes. The second and fourth Saturdays of each month may be declared holidays based on the academic calendar.
variations: Do we have school on Saturday?

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 1?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 1 is Rs.35,000. This includes tuition, smart class usage, and basic stationery kit. Uniforms and books are charged separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 2?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 2 is Rs.36,000. This includes tuition and smart class access. Books and uniforms are purchased separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 3?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 3 is Rs.37,000. Includes tuition and library access. Books and uniforms are extra.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 4?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 4 is Rs.38,000. Includes tuition, library, and sports activities. Books and uniforms are purchased separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 5?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 5 is Rs.42,000. This covers tuition, lab access, and library charges. Books and uniforms are purchased separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 6?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 6 is Rs.45,000. This includes tuition, computer lab access, and library. Books and uniforms are extra.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 7?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 7 is Rs.47,000. Includes lab access, library, and co-curricular activities. Books and uniforms are purchased separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 8?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 8 is Rs.50,000. Lab fees, library, and co-curricular activities are included. Books and uniforms are extra.
variations: What is the fee for Class 8?

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 9?
Fees.answer: The annual tuition fee for Class 9 is Rs.54,000. This includes lab access, library charges, and co-curricular fees. Books and uniforms are purchased separately.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 10?
Fees.answer: The annual fee for Class 10 is Rs.58,000. This includes board exam registration charges, practical lab fees, and pre-board examination fees.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 11?
Fees.answer: The annual fee for Class 11 is Rs.60,000. It includes tuition, lab/practical sessions, and career counselling. Books and uniforms are extra.

Fees.question: What is the annual fee for Class 12?
Fees.answer: The annual fee for Class 12 is Rs.62,000. It includes CBSE board registration, practicals, and career counselling sessions.

Fees.question: Can I pay fees in installments?
Fees.answer: Yes, the school allows fee payment in three installments: the first in April, the second in August, and the third in December. An installment form must be submitted to the accounts office.
variations: Can fees be paid in parts?

Fees.question: What happens if fees are paid late?
Fees.answer: A late fee of Rs.100 per day is charged if payment is not made by the due date. After 30 days of non-payment, the student's name may be struck off the rolls.
variations: What is the late fee penalty?
What if I miss fee deadline?
Fine for not paying fee on time?

Fees.question: What are the payment modes available?
Fees.answer: Fees can be paid online via the school portal using UPI, Net Banking, or Debit/Credit card. Cash and cheque payments are accepted at the accounts office between 9:00 AM and 1:00 PM.

Fees.question: Is there a fee concession or scholarship?
Fees.answer: Yes, the school offers fee concessions for students who score above 90% in the annual exam and for economically weaker sections under the RTE Act. Apply at the admin office before March 31st.

Fees.question: What is the admission fee?
Fees.answer: The one-time non-refundable admission registration fee is Rs.1,500. This is separate from the annual tuition fee and covers administrative and documentation charges.
variations: How much is the registration fee?

Fees.question: Is the security deposit refundable?
Fees.answer: Yes, a refundable caution deposit of Rs.5,000 is collected at the time of admission. It is returned after the student completes their schooling or leaves the school, provided all dues are cleared.
variations: Will I get back the deposit?

Academic.question: What subjects are taught in Class 9?
Academic.answer: Class 9 covers: Mathematics, Science (Physics, Chemistry, Biology), Social Science, English, Hindi, and one elective subject. Practical sessions are conducted for Science throughout the year.

Academic.question: What subjects are taught in Class 10?
Academic.answer: Class 10 covers: Mathematics, Science, Social Science, English, Hindi, and one optional subject. Practical examinations are conducted for Science.

Academic.question: What subjects are taught in Class 11?
Academic.answer: Class 11 offers three streams: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Maths/Biology, English), Commerce (Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, English), and Arts (History, Political Science, Geography, English). Students choose one stream at admission.
variations: What streams are available in Class 11?

Academic.question: What subjects are taught in Class 12?
Academic.answer: Class 12 follows the same stream chosen in Class 11: Science, Commerce, or Arts. CBSE board examinations are conducted in February-March for all core and elective subjects.

Academic.question: What subjects are taught in Class 8?
Academic.answer: Class 8 covers: Mathematics, Science, Social Science, English, Hindi, Sanskrit, and Computer Science.

Academic.question: When are the final exams scheduled?
Academic.answer: Annual final examinations for Classes 1 to 9 are held in March. CBSE board examinations for Class 10 and Class 12 begin in February as per the CBSE schedule.

Academic.question: What is the grading system?
Academic.answer: The school follows the CBSE grading system: A1 (91-100), A2 (81-90), B1 (71-80), B2 (61-70), C1 (51-60), C2 (41-50), D (33-40), and E below 33.

Academic.question: What co-curricular activities are available?
Academic.answer: The school offers cricket, football, chess, dance, music, art and craft, debate club, science club, and NCC. Students are encouraged to participate in at least one activity.

Academic.question: What board does the school follow?
Academic.answer: The school is affiliated with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), New Delhi. Our affiliation number is XXXXXXX.

Academic.question: When are results declared?
Academic.answer: Results for term exams are declared within two weeks of the last exam. Annual results are published in April. Students can check results on the school portal or collect physical mark sheets from the office.
variations: When will I get my result?

Admissions.question: How can I apply for admission?
Admissions.answer: Admissions can be done online through the school's official website or in person at the school office. The process involves: (1) Collect the application form, (2) Submit with required documents, (3) Entrance test/interaction, and (4) Pay admission fee of Rs.1,500.

Admissions.question: When do admissions open?
Admissions.answer: Admissions open every year in January for the upcoming academic session starting in June. Early applications are encouraged as seats are limited.
variations: When can I apply for admission?

Admissions.question: What documents are required for admission?
Admissions.answer: Required documents include: Birth certificate, last year's report card or Transfer Certificate (TC), Aadhaar card of parent and child, passport-size photographs (4), and caste/income certificate if applying under RTE.

Admissions.question: What is the age criteria for Class 1?
Admissions.answer: A child must be at least 6 years old as of June 1st of the admission year to be eligible for Class 1 admission, as per CBSE guidelines.
variations: How old should my child be for Class 1?

Admissions.question: Are seats available under RTE?
Admissions.answer: Yes, 25% of seats in Class 1 are reserved under the Right to Education Act for economically weaker section students. Apply with income proof before February 28th.

Policies.question: What is the attendance requirement?
Policies.answer: As per CBSE norms, students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance to be eligible for the annual examinations. Students with less than 75% may be detained.
variations: How many days can I miss school?

Policies.question: What is the uniform policy?
Policies.answer: Students must wear the prescribed school uniform every day. Monday to Thursday: white shirt, navy blue trousers or skirt and school tie. Friday: House-colour PT uniform.

Policies.question: What is the mobile phone policy?
Policies.answer: Mobile phones are strictly prohibited inside the school premises for students. If a student is found with a phone, it will be confiscated and returned only to parents after a formal meeting.
variations: Can I carry phone to school?

Policies.question: What is the leave application procedure?
Policies.answer: For planned leave, submit an application in the school diary signed by a parent at least one day in advance. For medical leave, a doctor's certificate must be submitted upon return.

Policies.question: What is the anti-bullying policy?
Policies.answer: The school has a zero-tolerance policy for bullying, ragging, or discrimination. Any student found guilty of bullying will face disciplinary action including suspension.
variations: What if my child is bullied?

Policies.question: What is the discipline policy?
Policies.answer: Students are expected to maintain respectful behaviour. Repeated misconduct is addressed through: verbal warning, parent meeting, and suspension.

Facilities.question: Is transport available?
Facilities.answer: Yes, the school provides bus transport covering major routes across the city and nearby localities. A route map is available at the admin office and on the school website.
variations: Can my child take the school bus?

Facilities.question: What is the transport fee?
Facilities.answer: Transport fees vary by distance: Rs.10,000 per year for routes within 5 km and Rs.20,000 per year for routes between 5 to 15 km. This is payable annually or in two installments.

Facilities.question: Does the school provide lunch?
Facilities.answer: Yes, a nutritious optional meal plan is available through the school canteen. A monthly meal subscription costs Rs.1,200. Students can also bring home-cooked lunch.

Facilities.question: Is there a library?
Facilities.answer: Yes, the school has a well-stocked library with over 10,000 books, including NCERT textbooks, reference books, encyclopaedias, and children's literature. Library cards are issued to all students.

Facilities.question: Is there a computer lab?
Facilities.answer: Yes, the school has a modern computer lab with 60 computers and high-speed internet access. Computer Science is a compulsory subject from Class 6 onwards.

Facilities.question: What sports facilities are available?
Facilities.answer: The school has a cricket ground, basketball court, volleyball court, and an indoor badminton court. Physical Education is a part of the daily schedule.

Facilities.question: Is there CCTV surveillance?
Facilities.answer: Yes, the entire school campus, including classrooms, corridors, and the main gate, is under 24/7 CCTV surveillance for student safety.

Facilities.question: Is there a medical room?
Facilities.answer: Yes, the school has a fully equipped medical room staffed by a trained nurse from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM on all working days. First aid is available for minor injuries and illnesses.

Staff.question: How can I contact the school office?
Staff.answer: You can reach the school office at +91-XXXXXXXXXX or email us at info@school.com. Office hours are Monday to Saturday, 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM.

Staff.question: Who is the principal?
Staff.answer: The Principal of the school is Mrs. Sharma. She holds a M.Ed. degree and has over 20 years of experience in education. You can schedule an appointment through the admin office.
variations: Who is the head of school?

Staff.question: How do I contact my child's class teacher?
Staff.answer: Class teacher contact details are shared at the start of the academic year. You may also contact them via the school app's messaging feature or request a meeting through the admin desk.
variations: How to talk to class teacher?

Staff.question: Who do I contact for fee-related issues?
Staff.answer: For all fee-related queries, contact the Accounts Office directly at accounts@school.com or visit Room 3 on the ground floor between 9:00 AM and 1:00 PM on working days.

HR.question: How many casual leaves do PRT teachers get?
HR.answer: PRT (Primary Teachers) are granted 12 Casual Leaves per academic year. Earned Leaves are calculated at 1 per month and can be accumulated up to a maximum of 30 days.
variations: How many CLs for primary teachers?
How many days leave for teachers?

HR.question: When are staff salaries credited?
HR.answer: Staff salaries are disbursed on the last working day of every month directly to their salary accounts via NEFT. Payslips are available on the staff portal.

HR.question: What is the maternity leave policy?
HR.answer: Female staff are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave as per the Maternity Benefit Act. An application must be submitted at least 60 days before the expected date.

HR.question: What is the protocol for summer vacation for non-teaching staff?
HR.answer: Non-teaching staff including peons, lab assistants, and administrative staff operate on a rotational duty schedule during the May-June summer vacation.

HR.question: How do I apply for earned leave?
HR.answer: Earned leave applications must be submitted to the principal's office at least 3 working days in advance through the school's leave management portal or in hard copy via the admin desk.

Health.question: Is there a school nurse or medical room?
Health.answer: Yes, the school has a fully equipped medical room staffed by a trained nurse from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM on all working days. First aid is available for minor injuries and illnesses.

Health.question: What happens in a medical emergency at school?
Health.answer: In case of a medical emergency, the school nurse is called immediately. Parents are notified within 10 minutes. If required, the student is taken to the nearest empanelled hospital. Emergency contact numbers are on file for every student.
variations: What to do if child is sick in school?

Health.question: Is there a health check-up for students?
Health.answer: Yes, an annual health and dental check-up camp is organized in August for all students in collaboration with a local hospital. Parents receive a health report card for their child.

Health.question: What is the policy for sick students attending school?
Health.answer: Students with fever, contagious illness, or Covid-like symptoms must not attend school. A medical certificate is required for absence of more than 3 days. The school reserves the right to send home an unwell student.
variations: Can sick student come to school?

Health.question: Does the school have a tie-up with any hospital?
Health.answer: Yes, the school has a tie-up with City Care Hospital, 2 km from campus. Emergency cases are taken there. The school also has ambulance access on call.

Events.question: When is Annual Day celebrated?
Events.answer: Annual Day is celebrated every year in the month of December. It features cultural performances, prize distribution, and a special address by the principal. All parents are invited.

Events.question: When is Sports Day held?
Events.answer: Sports Day is held in January every year on the school grounds. Students from all classes participate in track and field events, relay races, and team sports. Parents are welcome to attend.

Events.question: Does the school participate in inter-school competitions?
Events.answer: Yes, the school actively participates in inter-school competitions for debate, science, sports, quiz, painting, and dance at the district and state levels. Selected students receive coaching from teachers.

Events.question: Is there a Science Exhibition?
Events.answer: Yes, the school organizes an annual Science and Innovation Exhibition in October. Students from Class 6 to 12 submit projects. Outstanding projects are sent to district-level exhibitions.

Events.question: Does the school celebrate Independence Day and Republic Day?
Events.answer: Yes, Independence Day (August 15) and Republic Day (January 26) are celebrated with great enthusiasm. Flag hoisting, cultural programs, and prizes for patriotic competitions are organized.

Events.question: Is there a farewell for Class 12 students?
Events.answer: Yes, the school organizes a farewell ceremony for Class 12 students in March, hosted by Class 11 students. It includes cultural performances, mementos, and a message from the principal.

ParentPortal.question: How do I access the parent portal?
ParentPortal.answer: The parent portal is accessible at portal.school.com. Log in using the credentials provided at the time of admission. You can view attendance, fee receipts, exam results, and school notices.
variations: How to login to parent portal?
How to see child's attendance online?

ParentPortal.question: How do I download my child's fee receipt?
ParentPortal.answer: Fee receipts are available on the parent portal under the Payments section. Log in, select your child's profile, click on Fee History, and download the PDF receipt for any payment.

ParentPortal.question: How can I check my child's attendance online?
ParentPortal.answer: Attendance records are updated daily on the parent portal and mobile app. Log in and navigate to the Attendance section to view daily, weekly, or monthly attendance for your child.

ParentPortal.question: Is there a mobile app for parents?
ParentPortal.answer: Yes, the school has an official mobile app available on Android and iOS. It provides real-time notifications, attendance updates, homework, circulars, and fee payment in one place.

ParentPortal.question: How do I give feedback to the school?
ParentPortal.answer: Parents can submit feedback through the parent portal under the Feedback section, by email at feedback@school.com, or by dropping a written note at the admin office.

Counseling.question: Is there a student counselor in school?
Counseling.answer: Yes, the school has a trained professional counselor available every Tuesday and Thursday from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. Students can visit voluntarily or be referred by their class teacher.

Counseling.question: How can a student join the Debate Club?
Counseling.answer: Debate Club selections happen in July every year. Students from Class 8 onwards can apply by submitting a short speech to the English department. Practice sessions happen every Friday.

Counseling.question: Is there an NCC unit in the school?
Counseling.answer: Yes, the school has an NCC unit open to students from Class 9 onwards. Enrollment happens in June-July. NCC provides leadership, discipline training, and can benefit college admissions.

Counseling.question: Is there a Student Council?
Counseling.answer: Yes, the school has an elected Student Council consisting of a Head Boy, Head Girl, and class representatives. Elections are held every July. Any student from Class 9-12 can contest.

Counseling.question: What creative clubs does the school have?
Counseling.answer: The school has Art Club, Music Club, Drama Club, Photography Club, and Coding Club. Each club meets once a week and showcases work at the Annual Day and Science Exhibition.

Safety.question: What fire safety measures does the school have?
Safety.answer: The school has fire extinguishers on every floor, smoke detectors in labs, and clearly marked emergency exits. Fire safety drills are conducted every semester for all students and staff.

Safety.question: Is drinking water safe in school?
Safety.answer: The school provides RO-purified drinking water at multiple points across the campus. Water quality is tested monthly by a certified lab. Students are encouraged to carry personal reusable bottles.

Safety.question: What is the procedure for student pick-up and drop-off?
Safety.answer: Students must be picked up from the school gate only by registered guardians. ID verification is done by security. No child is handed over to an unknown person. Parents must update guardian details in the app.

Safety.question: What is the school's anti-ragging and bullying policy?
Safety.answer: The school has a zero-tolerance policy against ragging, bullying, and discrimination. A dedicated Anti-Bullying Committee meets monthly. Students can report anonymously via the portal or a suggestion box outside the principal's office.

Safety.question: Is there a lost and found in school?
Safety.answer: Yes, the school maintains a lost and found register at the admin office. Items found on campus are logged and kept for 30 days. Unclaimed items are donated to charity.
